summary: The SRY gene on the Y chromosome directs testicular development; in its absence, the gonad develops into an ovary.
The SRY gene on the X chromosome directs ovarian development.
Gonadal differentiation is determined solely by the presence of testosterone.
The menstrual cycle consists of follicular phase (days 1-14), ovulation (day 14), and luteal phase (days 15-28).
The menstrual cycle consists of luteal phase (days 1-14) and follicular phase (days 15-28).
Ovulation occurs on day 7 of the menstrual cycle.
The LH surge triggers ovulation, and the ruptured follicle becomes the corpus luteum which secretes progesterone.
The FSH surge triggers ovulation and the corpus luteum secretes oestrogen.
Ovulation is triggered by a rise in progesterone levels.
Testosterone is produced by Leydig (interstitial) cells under LH stimulation.
Testosterone is produced by Sertoli cells under FSH stimulation.
Testosterone is produced by the adrenal cortex exclusively.
Sertoli cells form the blood-testis barrier and support spermatogenesis under FSH stimulation.
Leydig cells form the blood-testis barrier and support spermatogenesis.
Sertoli cells produce testosterone under LH stimulation.
The combined oral contraceptive pill primarily prevents ovulation by inhibiting the LH surge.
The combined OCP works exclusively by thickening cervical mucus.
The combined OCP stimulates the LH surge to prevent implantation.
If fertilisation does not occur, the corpus luteum degenerates after about 14 days, causing progesterone to fall and menstruation to begin.
The corpus luteum persists for the entire cycle regardless of fertilisation.
Menstruation is triggered by a rise in oestrogen from the corpus luteum.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Revise hormonal regulation of reproduction.
